problems.title: Nozzle Reaction: 185 GPM Fog
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying a 1.75-inch attack line with a fog nozzle flowing 185 GPM at 50 PSI nozzle pressure. What is the nozzle reaction?
problems.instructorExplanation: This is a fog nozzle reaction problem, not a pump-pressure problem. Use NR = 0.0505 × GPM × √NP. With 185 GPM at 50 PSI nozzle pressure, nozzle reaction is 66 lbs.
problems.explainMistake: Common mistakes include using the pump-pressure formula instead of the nozzle reaction formula, using the smooth bore formula on a fog nozzle, using pump pressure instead of nozzle pressure, or rounding too early.

problems.title: Nozzle Reaction: 265 GPM Fog
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying a fog nozzle flowing 265 GPM at 50 PSI nozzle pressure. What is the nozzle reaction?
problems.instructorExplanation: This is a fog nozzle reaction problem, not a pump-pressure problem. Use NR = 0.0505 × GPM × √NP. With 265 GPM at 50 PSI nozzle pressure, nozzle reaction is 95 lbs.
problems.explainMistake: Common mistakes include using the pump-pressure formula instead of the nozzle reaction formula, using the smooth bore formula on a fog nozzle, using pump pressure instead of nozzle pressure, or rounding too early.

problems.title: Nozzle Reaction: 15/16-inch Smooth Bore
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying a 15/16-inch smooth bore nozzle operating at 50 PSI nozzle pressure. What is the nozzle reaction?
formulaBreakdown: Smooth bore nozzle reaction: NR = 1.57 × diameter² × NP
problems.instructorExplanation: This is a smooth bore nozzle reaction problem, not a pump-pressure problem. Use NR = 1.57 × diameter² × NP. With a 15/16-inch smooth bore at 50 PSI nozzle pressure, nozzle reaction is 69 lbs, rounded to 70 lbs.
problems.explainMistake: Common mistakes include using the pump-pressure formula instead of the nozzle reaction formula, using the fog formula on a smooth bore, forgetting to square the smooth bore diameter, using pump pressure instead of nozzle pressure, or rounding too early.

problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying a 1 1/8-inch smooth bore nozzle operating at 50 PSI nozzle pressure. What is the nozzle reaction?
formulaBreakdown: Smooth bore nozzle reaction: NR = 1.57 × diameter² × NP
problems.instructorExplanation: This is a smooth bore nozzle reaction problem, not a pump-pressure problem. Use NR = 1.57 × diameter² × NP. With a 1 1/8-inch smooth bore at 50 PSI nozzle pressure, nozzle reaction is 99 lbs, rounded to 100 lbs.
problems.explainMistake: Common mistakes include using the pump-pressure formula instead of the nozzle reaction formula, using the fog formula on a smooth bore, forgetting to square the smooth bore diameter, using pump pressure instead of nozzle pressure, or rounding too early.

problems.title: Nozzle Reaction: Deck Gun 1 1/2-inch Smooth Bore
problems.question: Engine 181 is operating a deck gun with a 1 1/2-inch smooth bore tip at 80 PSI nozzle pressure. What is the nozzle reaction?
formulaBreakdown: Smooth bore nozzle reaction: NR = 1.57 × diameter² × NP
problems.instructorExplanation: This is a deck gun smooth bore nozzle reaction problem, not a pump-pressure problem. Use NR = 1.57 × diameter² × NP. With a 1 1/2-inch smooth bore deck gun tip at 80 PSI nozzle pressure, nozzle reaction is 283 lbs, rounded to 285 lbs.
problems.explainMistake: Common mistakes include using the pump-pressure formula instead of the nozzle reaction formula, using the fog formula on a smooth bore, forgetting to square the smooth bore diameter, using pump pressure instead of nozzle pressure, or rounding too early.
